conversations.title: Beyond Earth
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1b and 1c

lines.speaker: news_reader
lines.text: China's new space programme was made public today. Exploration of the planet Mars and the moon will be continued. China's Zhurong rover successfully landed on Mars in 2021, and it is hoped that a manned mission to the moon will follow around 2030.

lines.speaker: news_reader
lines.text: China has announced that more astronauts will be sent to the Tiangong Space Station. Since the Space Station was completed in 2022, it has been used to do research on biology, medicine, and materials. It will also continue its cooperation with Germany, Italy, and Russia on scientific research.

lines.speaker: news_reader
lines.text: Hello! We have more news about space telescopes. The China Space Station Telescope (CSST), also known as Xuntian, will be sent to space around 2026. This huge and modern telescope weighs about 15,550 kilograms. It is designed to move along with Tiangong space station, so it can be more easily maintained and repaired. It is expected to help people explore more areas in space.

conversations.instruction: Listen to the news report. Complete your notes about China’s space programme.

questions.prompt: When did China’s Zhurong rover land on Mars?
When was the Tiangong Space Station completed?
When will the CSST (Xuntian) telescope be sent to space?

conversations.title: Beyond Earth
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 2a, 2b and 2c

lines.speaker: ms_wang
lines.text: All right. So, we've done some experiments. Now it's time for questions.

lines.speaker: anna
lines.text: Hi, Ms Wang! I want to know how it feels to be weightless.

lines.speaker: ms_wang
lines.text: Oh, it's so much fun! I can turn around easily! You see, I can move everywhere by just pushing against the wall!

lines.speaker: mike
lines.text: Ms Wang, what does Earth look like from the space station?

lines.speaker: ms_wang
lines.text: It looks like a huge shining ball. It's really beautiful.

lines.speaker: julie
lines.text: Ms Wang, I want to know how you eat up there.

lines.speaker: ms_wang
lines.text: Nice question. Well, all our food is prepared on Earth and kept in bags. We have over 120 dishes to choose from.

lines.speaker: david
lines.text: What's difficult about living in space?

lines.speaker: ms_wang
lines.text: Well, it's a little hard to fall asleep. We have to be tied down so we don't fly away!

lines.speaker: li_hua
lines.text: Do you get bored? Will you be visited by other astronauts?

lines.speaker: ms_wang
lines.text: It's never boring in space. Actually, two astronauts will arrive next month. It's very exciting.

conversations.instruction: Listen to students’ questions for Ms Wang on the space station. Answer the questions.

questions.prompt: How does Ms Wang describe being weightless?
options: Fun — she can turn and move by pushing against the wall

questions.prompt: What does Earth look like from the space station?
Why do astronauts have to be tied down to sleep?
